name: Kovu
story: Kovu, the Spirit of Constant Flow. Kovu is not a spirit of stillness, but of transition. He represents the moment of change,like a sprout breaking through the soil or a river turning a corner. His body is captured in a fluid, reclining curve, showing that true strength lies in flexibility and the ability to adapt to the ever-changing world. Kovu is a powerful companion for your tea ceremony if you seek inspiration, growth, or a reminder that life is a beautiful, never-ending movement.

name: Mura
story: Mura, the Spirit of the Night Forest. Mura is a silent guardian who appears when the moon is high. On his head, he carries a small crescent moon, a symbol of light that guides him through the darkness. His body is covered with hand-carved patterns of ancient, leafless branches, making him look like a part of an old winter tree. Mura doesn't need leaves to be beautiful; he represents the deep strength of roots and the quiet peace of the cold woods. He is a perfect companion for late-night tea sessions or quiet moments of meditation. He brings a calm, grounded energy to your tea tray.

name: Solas
story: Solas, the Voice of the Embers. Part of a small circle of spirits who gather around the sacred forest fire, Solas is the one who keeps the ancient melodies alive. He sings songs that only the trees and the rising steam can truly understand. Hand-sculpted from dark, textured clay, Solas features flowing spiral engravings on his sides,symbols of sound waves and the eternal rhythm of nature. A bright mystical rune is etched on his back, acting as a tuning fork for the energy of your tea space. He is the perfect companion for those who find music in the ritual of brewing. Whether you are listening to the whistle of the kettle or the quiet pour of water, Solas stands as a reminder to find your own voice and melody in the everyday.

name: Pio
story: Pio, the Heart of the Forest Choir. The final member of the campfire circle, Pio is the embodiment of kindness and universal harmony. While others sing or dream, Pio simply is. His energy is so warm and welcoming that the forest birds treat him as their favorite resting place, perching on his shoulders and even nesting on his head. He brings a sense of gentle companionship to your tea ceremony, reminding us that when we are at peace, the whole world wants to be near us.

name: Kitsune
story: Kitsune, a mischievous nine-tailed kitsune tea pet who arrives with an unexpected companion: a tiny chicken perched on her head, as if it's the most natural crown in the world. She adores good tea (the kind that lingers on the tongue) and lives for interesting conversations, secrets, stories, bold opinions, laughter that warms the cups.

name: Pip
story: Pip, the Silent Perch. Pip is a spirit of such immense stillness and kindness that even the shiest forest birds feel safe enough to rest on him.

name: Nox
story: Nox, the Star-Sleeper. While the other spirits guard the forest, Nox is the keeper of its dreams. He only wakes when the moon is high, but for your tea ceremony he offers a quiet, grounding presence. He does not demand attention; he simply shares his calm with you.

name: Runehorn Sentinel
story: Somewhere between smoke, tea steam, and old forest silence, the Runehorn Sentinel keeps watch. This handmade unglazed ceramic tea pet is a small horned guardian spirit, quiet, grounded, and oddly comforting on a tea table. Its dark, matte surface and carved details feel like something found in a forgotten shrine or tucked into a wooden box of charms. Place it beside your teapot, and it becomes part of the ritual: a steady presence while the kettle sings and the world slows down.

name: Six-Eyed Spirit Bird
story: This design is inspired by modern Korean animation folklore. Bring the mischievous charm of Korean myths and art to your tea table with this handmade Magpie tea pet. Known as the snarky, six-eyed familiar, she is the perfect companion for those who like their tea with a side of mystery and a hint of demon-hunting magic. This magpie represents playful cunning and loyalty. In your tea ceremony, she acts as a Watcher of the Brew, ensuring every steep is perfect.

name: Master of the Forest
story: He arrived the way quiet things do, without an entrance, without a warning. Master of the Forest is a handmade unglazed ceramic tea pet shaped like a calm horned spirit, wearing a pale skull mask like a winter moon over dark earth. He rests low and still, as if he's been here longer than the table itself, listening to the kettle and the weather. This one isn't playful, and he isn't in a hurry. He has the kind of presence that makes a room feel calmer, a patient, watchful guardian who prefers silence to noise and meaning to small talk. Set him on your tray and he becomes part of the ritual: a steady shadow at the edge of lamplight, a companion for late sessions, rainy mornings, and conversations that don't need to be rushed. Feed him a little tea, let the warmth pass over him, and watch his surface slowly change, subtle, patient, earned.

name: Marl
story: Marl, the Beauty Collector. Marl is a flower slug with a patient eye for beauty. He slowly gathers the delicate things most creatures pass by: fallen petals, pale blossoms, soft shadows, tiny marks in stone, and the gentle shapes left by time. Do you love to collect small beautiful things, enjoy botanical details, like your tea rituals to feel personal, poetic, and a little unusual?

name: Koda
story: Koda The Root-Dreamer is a spirit of the deep woods who has sat in silence for centuries. He is so still and peaceful that tiny forest mushrooms have chosen him as their home. He doesn't hurry; he reminds us that growth takes time and that there is beauty in stillness.

name: Noru
story: Noru is a flying whale spirit who carries little mountains across skies. He is drawn to distant horizons, half-planned journeys, secret maps, and the feeling that somewhere out there another beautiful place is waiting to be found. He drifts to know what is waiting ahead, carrying landscapes, wishes, and unfinished dreams as if they were precious cargo. Keep him near during tea as a small companion for planning future travels, remembering places you miss, or letting your mind wander beyond the room for a while.

name: Vesper
mood: Small raven of the night sky.
story: Vesper, the Night Compass. Vesper is a night raven spirit who appears when the world goes to sleep and the light begins to fade. He is observant, steady, and thoughtful, a companion for evening tea, focused moments, and times when you need to find your own direction. Keep him near your tea tray as a small reminder to slow down, look closer, and trust the small signs.

name: Bracken
story: Bracken, the Keeper of Small Secrets. Bracken is a woodland spirit who listens more than he speaks. He knows where the old roots hide their stories, where mushrooms appear after rain, and which fern leaves are best for tucking away tiny secrets. Share your tea with him, and he will keep watch over the thoughts, wishes, and little mysteries of your tea table. You can share your own little secrets with Bracken during tea, or let him inspire fantastic and magical stories while he keeps watch over your tea table.

name: Hob
story: Hob, the Tea-Table Master Hob is a good-natured little household spirit who believes that every cup, spoon, leaf, and pause should have its proper place. He is the kind of companion who seems to know when the tea needs more time, when the table needs clearing, and when it is better to stop fussing and simply sit for a moment. He is practical, kind, and opinionated. Not in a dramatic way actually, but in the way of someone who has seen many small domestic disasters and knows exactly how they could have been avoided. Keep him near during tea as a tiny adviser for ordinary rituals, important decisions, and companionable silence. Hob feels like a small house spirit who wandered onto the tea tray and immediately decided he could turn the whole arrangement into a cozy magic ritual.

name: Queen of Ashes
story: Queen of Ashes. She was created for light, beauty, and creation. Elegant and fragile, she loved her world and ruled it with tenderness. But when darkness fell upon her kingdom, she did not run. Like a beautiful rose, she grew thorns of cold, forged silver. She traded her delicate rings for heavy armor and took up a two-handed sword. She lost everything. But she'll never give up, continuing to fight for her beliefs. She is the perfect balance of fierce femininity and ruthless strength. Her dusty pink dress and fine lace are a memory of the tenderness she still holds inside. The heavy armor and sword are a promise that she will never step back. She didn't seek the war, but she will fight for all she loves. Cold. Resolute. Lethally beautiful.

name: Iron Harlequin
story: Echo of the Throne. The court took him for a funny little freak, never realizing the motley cap was the perfect vantage point for a cold, piercing mind. To everyone he was a silent laughingstock; to the Queen, the only advisor whose words were never poisoned by flattery. When the kingdom turned to ash, he slipped into her shadow and stayed, following her through the ruined lands, calm and calculating, his loyalty absolute and in need of no words.

name: Gentleman of the Lower City
mood: A charming thief of the gas-lit city.
story: The yellow glow of gas lamps struggles through the thick factory smog. A police whistle shrieks below, but on the wrought-iron cornice absolute calm prevails. An elegant figure in a dapper vest casually tosses a heavy coin purse, a flash of lightning catching the mocking smirk behind his brass goggles before he melts into the labyrinth of steam. In the Upper City archives his file is just wild guesses and complaints from influential aristocrats.

name: The Librarian
story: She is a poseable textile and ceramic doll, inspired by old libraries, forgotten languages, and the soft glow of candlelight. She is not just a doll, she is a story in physical form.

name: Master of the Forest
mood: Old god of the city's edges.
story: Who is he: An old keeper of wild places who adapted to the modern city without becoming part of it. Where he is usually found: Vacant lots, overgrown rail lines, courtyards, abandoned sites, and the edges of places people stopped noticing. Temperament: Calm, stubborn, curious, and quietly amused.

name: The Dreamer
story: He is an introverted street artist, who listens to lo-fi beats through his headphones and speaks to the world with spray paint. Handcrafted with care, this character doll is perfect for collectors, lovers of slow living, and those who connect with stories told without words.

name: The Observer
story: In a loud city there is one very quiet elf. He drinks hot tea, listens to psy trance, and watches the world go by in silence. Calm and thoughtful, he likes to observe far more than he speaks.

name: The Garden Snail
story: A whimsical cottagecore wanderer who carries his whole world on his back. More than a doll, he is a small handcrafted sculpture of the slow and cheerful beauty of the natural world, in no rush to be anywhere at all.

name: The Roastery Kodama
vibe: The hyperactive soulmate who never sleeps and charges everyone around him with electric en
story: WHO IS HE? The Roastery Kodama: The Resonance of Awakening He is concentrated life energy, the invisible force that shakes the city out of its slumber. Once, this forest spirit matured in the warm gloom of tropical lands, for years soaking up the juices from the roots of wild coffee groves. Since moving to the megalopolis, he has quickly found his new power spots: the fragrant, sun-warmed corners of roasting houses and specialty coffee shops. He has become the Roastery Kodama, a spirit-resonator who sincerely adores the morning rush, the hum of industrial roasters, and the thick, intoxicating scent of a fresh grind. A companion to early birds and midnight thinkers. He is a reminder: to truly wake up, find your voice, and embrace a new day, you must simply shed your old, outgrown shell.

name: Skyline Nomad
story: WHO IS HE? He dwells where the city's concrete constraints end and the open expanse begins. The Skyline Nomad is a spirit of the heights, driven by a physical need for the wind in his face and constant motion. He shares the upper realm with the birds and effortlessly balances on the edges of the abyss just for the pure, intoxicating feeling of freedom. If you've ever felt the urge to escape the suffocating noise of the streets and find peace on a high, wind-swept roof, this little nomad is your perfect companion. He is a listener for those who find comfort and freedom in the heights.
